title: Quantum Foundations in Lean: Formal Companion to One State, Many Perspectives
description: A Lean 4 and Mathlib formal companion to the manuscript "One State, Many Perspectives: Branch Structure and Born Weights in Everettian Quantum Mechanics" (submitted to Foundations of Physics). Contains machine-checked formalizations of Naimark dilation, Wigner's theorem, Uhlhorn-type uniqueness, the grain-coherence-to-Born representation theorem, an effect-perspective route to the Born rule via Busch's theorem (including its Naimark realization), Kent's contrary-inferences construction, Riedel's branch-decomposition theorem with its record-induced Born-weight connection, redundant-record interference-complexity lower bounds with robust and dynamical persistence extensions, and restricted record-sector uniqueness (C15) with its quantitative weight-stability extensions (C17/C17b).
